title: Library Registry — publish reusable AI hooks, skills, and MCP servers
scope: v1 ships the shared library_registry module + manifest schema + ai_hooks/mcp_register/skills consumer wiring. Cosign sig verification and `jarvy library {sync,list,show,clean}` subcommand deferred to phases 5-6.
completionNote: src/library_registry/ module shipped: manifest (tagged kind: ai_hook|mcp_server|skill), HTTPS-bounded fetch, on-disk cache at ~/.jarvy/library.d/<sha256-of-url>/, in-process resolver. Wired into [ai_hooks] library_sources (built-in LIBRARY wins, library fallback resolves via use="..."), [mcp_register] library_sources (use="..." + local override semantics on env/args), [skills] full pipeline (sha256-verified SKILL.md fetch + .jarvy-skill.json sidecar). Remote-config trust gate uniform across all three consumers via library_registry::check_origin. docs/library-registry.md + docs/skills.md ship.
follow_up: Public reference library (community-maintained manifest of common hooks)
reconciled: 2026-07-08: companion fetch (ai_hook bash_url/powershell_url + skill companion_files, sha-verified, content-addressed cache, path-safety validated) MERGED to main via feat/library-companion-fetch, with an integration test harness. Remaining follow_up: cosign phase-5 hardening + public reference library (both post-v1).
